back: Trying to retrieve information from memory without looking at notes; it strengthens long-term retention.
The illusion of knowing caused by rereading or highlighting without testing recall.
A set of weak items you repeatedly test and rework until recall improves.
Turning abstract information into memorable mental images to improve encoding.
A framework such as a story, chunk, or memory palace that links items in recall order.
Taking in information through reading, watching, or listening.
Processing information through retrieval, mapping, critique, practice, and rehearsal.
How-to knowledge; best learned through early and repeated practice.
Principles and relationships; best learned by mapping connections into a coherent model.
Remembering what matters in a form you can apply to solve problems, not just recite.
